mods.name: Performance Intake
mods.description: An upgraded intake for the FA20 boxer engine improves throttle response and adds the distinctive boxer induction sound.
mods.installNotes: Remove stock airbox. Install new intake tube and filter. Ensure MAF sensor is properly seated. 15 minutes. No tune required for most intakes.

mods.name: Catback Exhaust
mods.description: The stock BRZ exhaust is quiet and restrictive. An aftermarket catback improves the boxer sound and provides minor power gains.
mods.installNotes: Bolt-on from the over-pipe back. Dual exit or single exit options. Hangs on factory mounts. No cutting. 45 minutes on jack stands.

mods.name: Lowering Springs
mods.description: Lower the BRZ by 20–25mm for a more aggressive stance and improved cornering with a lower center of gravity.
mods.partNumbers: Swift Spec-R Springs 4T905, Eibach Pro-Kit E10-72-014-01-22
mods.installNotes: Spring compressor required. Replace all four corners. Front requires removing the strut assembly. Alignment required. Ride quality remains comfortable for daily use.

mods.name: UEL Header
mods.description: An unequal-length header adds the classic Subaru boxer rumble while improving exhaust flow for mid-range torque gains on the FA20.
mods.installNotes: Access from below. Remove heat shields and stock manifold. Anti-seize on all studs. Requires ECU tune to optimize timing and clear secondary cat CEL. Gains 10–15hp mid-range. 3 hours.

mods.name: ECU Tune (EcuTek/OFT)
mods.description: An ECU tune addresses the stock torque dip, improves throttle response, and adds 15–25hp when paired with header and exhaust modifications.
mods.partNumbers: EcuTek ProECU License, Openflash Tablet OFT2
mods.installNotes: Flash via OBD-II. Select map matching your hardware level. Eliminates the factory torque dip between 3500–4500 RPM. Requires 93 octane. Custom dyno tune recommended for best results.

mods.name: Coilover Suspension
mods.description: The BRZ's chassis is its greatest asset. Proper coilovers with adjustable damping make it one of the best-handling cars under $50K.
mods.installNotes: Full strut replacement. 7k/5k spring rates are ideal for dual-purpose street/track. Set ride height 15–20mm below stock. The BRZ is extremely responsive to suspension changes. Full alignment mandatory.

mods.name: Forced Induction (Turbo Kit)
mods.description: A turbo kit addresses the BRZ's main weakness — a lack of power. Expect 250–300whp from a bolt-on kit on stock internals.
mods.partNumbers: Jackson Racing C38 Supercharger, Full-Race Turbo Kit
mods.installNotes: Comprehensive kit includes turbo/supercharger, intercooler, fuel system upgrades, and oil lines. Requires custom EcuTek tune. Stock internals handle 300whp on proper tune. Upgraded clutch needed. 12+ hours.

mods.name: Lightweight Wheels and Tires
mods.description: The BRZ weighs only 2,800 lbs — every pound of unsprung weight reduction is immediately felt in acceleration and handling response.
mods.installNotes: Direct bolt-on. 17x9 with 245-width tires is the sweet spot. Some fender rolling may be needed at lower ride heights. Torque lugs to 89 ft-lbs.
